mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals monetary easing, reducing discount rates for equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods of FEDFUNDS decline are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in unemployment rate above 9.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in high UNRATE regimes exceed 15% annualized or show no correlation with unemployment trends (Pearson r < 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in housing starts (HOUST) above 600k units signals strong consumer demand, driving S&P 500 positive returns over 5 trading days via sectoral demand spillovers.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in high HOUST regimes fall below 0.5% or show negative correlation with housing data (Pearson r < -0.2).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 93.5 signals weakening supply chain capacity, causing S&P 500 negative returns over 20 trading days via inflationary pressure.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in low INDPRO regimes exceed 10% annualized or show no correlation with industrial output (Pearson r < 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in CPI (CPIAUCSL) and FEDFUNDS signal disinflationary pressures, creating a 'bond-equity dual tailwind' that drives S&P 500 positive returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in dual-decline regimes show negative correlation with combined CPI-FEDFUNDS signal (Rank IC < -0.05) or exceed 20% annualized volatility.

mechanism_narrative: Positive GDP growth (GDPC1) above 2.5% combined with rising retail sales (RSAFS) over 3 months indicates robust demand, generating S&P 500 positive returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in high GDP-RSAFS regimes show negative correlation with demand indicators (Rank IC < -0.1) or fall below 5% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month decline in payroll employment (PAYEMS) below 100k combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE) above 9.0% signals labor market weakness, causing S&P 500 negative returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in weak labor regimes exceed 10% annualized or show no correlation with PAYEMS-UNRATE composite (Pearson r < 0.2).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) above 94.5 combined with falling unemployment (UNRATE) below 9.0% indicates supply-side expansion and labor market tightness, driving S&P 500 positive returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in high INDPRO-LOW UNRATE regimes show negative correlation with composite signal (Rank IC < -0.07) or fall below 7% annualized.
